sections_processed: Article 10: Policy Lapse and Grace Period

facts.fact: The insurer is Horizon Wealth Protection Insurance.
The policy number is HWP-IUL-2024-7290.
The date of issue is October 3, 2024.
The policy form is Equity-Indexed Universal Life.
The policy owner is Jessica Lynn Thompson.
The insured is Jessica Lynn Thompson.
Jessica Lynn Thompson's date of birth is January 25, 1980.
Jessica Lynn Thompson's age at issue is 44.
Jessica Lynn Thompson's gender is Female.
Jessica Lynn Thompson's underwriting class is Standard Plus Non-Tobacco.
The initial face amount is $500,000.
The death benefit option is Option 1 (Level).
The planned annual premium is $6,000.
The minimum premium to keep the policy in force varies by year.
The primary beneficiary is Matthew Scott Thompson (Spouse) at 100%.
The contingent beneficiary is Thompson Family Trust at 100%.
Index Account is defined as an account where interest credits are based on the performance of a stock market index.
Fixed Account is defined as an account earning a guaranteed fixed interest rate.
Cap Rate is defined as the maximum interest rate that can be credited to an index account in any crediting period.
Floor Rate is defined as the minimum interest rate (0%) that will be credited, protecting against market losses.
Participation Rate is defined as the percentage of index gain that is credited to the account.
Point-to-Point Method is defined as interest crediting based on index change from beginning to end of crediting period (annual).
The S&P 500 Index Account (Annual Point-to-Point) has a current cap of 10.5%, participation rate of 100%, and floor of 0%, with a crediting period of 1 year.
The NASDAQ-100 Index Account (Annual Point-to-Point) has a current cap of 9.0%, participation rate of 100%, and floor of 0%, with a crediting period of 1 year.
The Global Diversified Index Account has a current cap of 8.5%, participation rate of 100%, and floor of 0%, with a crediting period of 1 year.
The Fixed Account has a guaranteed interest rate of 3.0% annually with no cap and no floor variability.
The current allocation is: S&P 500 Index 50%, NASDAQ-100 Index 30%, Fixed Account 20%.
The policy owner may change allocations on any policy anniversary at no charge.
The first 4 reallocations per year are free; a $25 fee applies thereafter.
Cap rates are guaranteed to never be less than 4%.
Participation rates are guaranteed to never be less than 25%.
The fixed account interest rate is guaranteed to never be less than 2%.
Index interest is credited on each policy anniversary using the Point-to-Point method.
Index % Change = (Ending Index Value - Starting Index Value) / Starting Index Value.
Interest Credited = Index % Change x Participation Rate, subject to Cap and Floor.
If the S&P 500 Index started at 4,000, ended at 4,600 (15% gain) with 100% participation and 10.5% cap, interest credited would be 10.5% (capped).
If the index declines, the floor protects at 0%, so interest credited is 0% (no loss).
The policy owner never loses money due to market decline; the worst case is 0% interest for a year.
The minimum premium payment is $100 per payment.
The maximum premium is $50,000 per year (tax qualification limit).
The planned premium is $6,000 annually, recommended to maintain coverage to age 100 based on current assumptions.
The policy owner may skip premium payments as long as account value is sufficient to cover monthly deductions.
The premium load is 3.5% of each premium payment.
The Cost of Insurance (COI) is deducted monthly based on: (Death Benefit - Account Value) x Monthly COI Rate / 1,000.
The administrative charge is $12 per month.
Total monthly charges are deducted from account value on the 3rd of each month.
COI rates are guaranteed not to exceed rates shown in the policy schedule.
Under Option 1 (Level Death Benefit, currently selected), the death benefit equals $500,000 (specified amount) with lower ongoing charges.
Under Option 2 (Increasing Death Benefit), the death benefit equals the Specified Amount plus Account Value with higher ongoing charges.
The policy owner may change death benefit options on any anniversary with 60 days notice.
Evidence of insurability is required to increase coverage (change death benefit options).
Policy loans are available after 1 year.
The maximum policy loan is 90% of surrender value.
The loan interest rate is 4% annually (fixed).
Loaned amounts earn the fixed account rate of 3%.
The net cost of policy loans is 1% annually (4% loan rate minus 3% credited rate).
The minimum partial withdrawal is $500.
The maximum partial withdrawal is account value minus surrender charge.
The first 10 withdrawals are free; a $50 fee applies thereafter.
Under Option 1, partial withdrawals reduce the death benefit dollar-for-dollar.
Outstanding loans reduce the death benefit at time of claim.
Surrender charges apply in Years 1-12: Year 1: 10%, Year 2: 9%, Year 3: 8%, Year 4: 7%, Year 5: 6%, Year 6: 5%, Year 7: 4%, Year 8: 3%, Year 9: 2%, Year 10: 1%, Year 11: 0.5%, Year 12+: 0%.
Surrender charge is calculated as a percentage of premiums paid in the first 10 years.
Cash Surrender Value = Account Value - Surrender Charge - Outstanding Loans.
The policy owner may withdraw up to 10% of account value each year without surrender charge after Year 1 (free partial withdrawal provision).
A No Lapse Guarantee Rider is available as an optional rider but is NOT currently selected on this policy.
The No Lapse Guarantee Rider guarantees coverage will not lapse even if account value drops to zero, as long as minimum guaranteed premiums are paid.
If account value is insufficient to pay monthly deductions, the insurer will notify the policy owner at least 30 days before lapse.
The grace period is 61 days to pay sufficient premium to bring the policy back in force.
If premium is not received within the grace period, the policy lapses and any remaining surrender value is paid to the policy owner.
If the insured commits suicide within 2 years of issue or reinstatement, the insurer pays only premiums paid minus any withdrawals and charges, without interest.
The insurer may contest this policy for material misrepresentation for 2 years from the issue date.
This is NOT a securities product; index participation does not involve direct ownership of stocks or index funds.
Dividends from index components are NOT included in index performance calculations.
Cap rates, participation rates, and fixed account rates may change annually but will never go below guaranteed minimums.
Past index performance does not guarantee future results.
The policy owner cannot lose money due to negative index performance.
The policy owner does not participate in dividends of index components.
The customer service phone number is 1-888-HORIZON-1.
The company website is www.horizonwealthprotection.com.
The policy was executed at Chicago, Illinois.
The authorized signatory is the Chief Executive Officer of Horizon Wealth Protection Insurance.
The policy execution date is October 3, 2024.
Matthew Scott Thompson's relationship to the insured is Spouse.
The surrender charge schedule applies for the first 12 years of the policy.
